tool.name: makeprotseq
tool.summary: Create deterministic protein sequence records from a bounded random generator

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `makeprotseq` creates one or more deterministic protein sequence records from a canonical amino-acid alphabet and a documented seed. The v1 implementation is intentionally narrow and governed, prioritizing reproducible generated records over a broad randomization surface.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface requires an identifier prefix and an exact residue length. Optional flags control `--count`, `--seed`, and a shared description string attached to generated records.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits one or more FASTA protein records through the shared sequence output path. CLI summaries report the identifier prefix, length, count, deterministic seed, and output format.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `emboss-rs makeprotseq`. Validation currently covers deterministic seed behavior, identifier-prefix behavior, and protein output typing in the Rust tool and service layers.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The v1 surface supports only canonical amino-acid generation with exact-length output. It does not model residue frequencies, motifs, low-complexity constraints, or biologically informed composition targets.

artifacts.label: makeprotseq governed case note
artifacts.description: Repository-managed note describing the deterministic protein generation case used for the governed `makeprotseq` surface.

examples.title: Generate one deterministic protein record
examples.description: Builds one five-residue protein sequence record from a documented seed.

expected_outputs.label: Generated protein record
expected_outputs.description: One FASTA protein record named `made_prot` is emitted, and the shared result summary reports the deterministic seed and protein molecule kind.
